business_goals: Answer the 'Big Five Questions' regarding operator knowledge and action timing
Serve as a rapid prototyping facility for interoperability studies
Support evolving mission needs and incremental functionality extensions
technical_constraints: Must be compliant with High Level Architecture (HLA) standards
Must run on government-owned contractor-operated infrastructure
Must support human-in-control experiments with 'fog-of-war' realism

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: Documentation existed in Rational Rose/UML but was considered weak for capturing discrete events. Some architectural drawings caused stakeholder confusion and required real-time refinement.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: How are discrete simulation events represented in the static UML models?
Can you clarify the data flow in this architectural diagram to resolve stakeholder confusion?
How does the code reuse strategy align with the need for high-fidelity discrete event simulation?

hardware_view_description: Distributed simulation environment capable of hosting online, offsite, and multiple simultaneous games.
Networked command centers allowing participants to play from their own locations.
runtime_constraints: Must provide 'Margin' (running faster than real-time) to support future requirements.

questions: How does the client-server separation impact the latency requirements for the human-in-the-loop displays?
How does the publish/subscribe mechanism handle peak load during high-density missile scenarios?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: Simulation controller initiates simulation execution, starts subordinate processes, and runs from T=-infinity to T=0.
Change joint firing doctrine rule set in the automated weapon target assigner in time for the next game.
Add cruise missile infrastructure into next software release.
Reconfigure Wargame 2000 from running a theater ballistic missile defense simulation to a national missile defense simulation in one week.
Replace existing database with an object-oriented database within 5-10 staff years.

architectural_approaches.description: The system is designed as an HLA-compliant federation where individual simulation components (federates) interact through a Runtime Infrastructure (RTI). This supports the distributed nature of the wargaming environment.

addressed_attributes.rationale: HLA standards ensure that disparate simulation systems and command center interfaces can exchange data consistently.
The federated approach allows for incremental functionality extensions and the replacement of specific simulation modules (e.g., weapon target assigners) without system-wide impact.

architectural_approaches.description: Communication between simulation entities is handled via a publish-subscribe mechanism, likely mediated by the HLA RTI, where data providers publish state changes and consumers subscribe only to relevant updates.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Optimizes network bandwidth by ensuring that high-density data (like missile tracks) is only sent to interested nodes, helping meet 'Firm' real-time deadlines.
Decouples the producers of simulation data from the consumers, facilitating the addition of new sensor or weapon types.

architectural_approaches.description: The architecture separates simulation execution servers from distributed participant consoles (clients) located at various command centers to support remote play and 'fog-of-war' realism.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Distributes the processing load for human-in-the-loop displays and UI logic away from the central simulation engine.
Enables the system to isolate client-side failures from the core simulation execution.

architectural_approaches.description: The core simulation engine utilizes a discrete event model to manage state transitions and time progression, allowing for both real-time execution and 'faster than real-time' (Margin) processing.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Ensures event causality and timing are maintained within 10% of reference models like EADSIM.
Allows the simulation to skip periods of inactivity to support the 'Margin' requirement for future growth.

children.scenario_description: The system must process 1,000 concurrent missile tracks and update all federates within 100ms during peak simulation load.
The simulation engine must support a 'Margin' mode executing at 2x real-time speed for future growth scenarios.
Engagement simulation outcomes for standard wargaming scenarios must stay within a 10% margin of error compared to EADSIM reference data.
A new weapon system federate or sensor model is integrated into the HLA federation within 10 person-days of development.
A 3rd party command center console connects to the RTI and synchronizes its local world state with the federation within 1 second.
A distributed participant console fails or disconnects; the central simulation engine continues execution without data loss or interruption.

scenarios.scenario_text: Under peak load (1,000 concurrent missile tracks), the system must maintain a latency of less than 100ms for updates between federates.
The simulation engine must support a 'Margin' mode executing at 2x real-time speed for rapid scenario analysis.
Engagement simulation outcomes for standard wargaming scenarios must stay within a 10% margin of error compared to EADSIM reference data.
A developer can integrate a new weapon system federate or sensor model into the HLA federation within 10 person-days of development.
A 3rd party command center console connects to the RTI and synchronizes its local world state with the federation within 1 second.
A distributed participant console fails or disconnects; the central simulation engine continues execution without data loss or interruption.

scenario_reference.text: Under peak load (1,000 concurrent missile tracks), the system must maintain a latency of less than 100ms for updates between federates.

analysis_records.stimulus: 1,000 concurrent missile tracks generating state updates
architectural_decisions: HLA Runtime Infrastructure (RTI) for data distribution
analysis_records.reasoning: The RTI manages all data exchange. While Pub-Sub reduces unnecessary traffic, the RTI itself introduces a serialization and management overhead that scales with the number of objects (tracks) and subscribers.

risks.rationale: If the selected RTI implementation uses a centralized component for message routing, 1,000 tracks may exceed the throughput capacity, causing latency spikes beyond 100ms.

non_risks.rationale: The use of HLA Data Distribution Management (DDM) ensures that federates only receive updates for tracks within their interest area, significantly reducing client-side processing load.

scenario_reference.text: A developer can integrate a new weapon system federate or sensor model into the HLA federation within 10 person-days of development.

analysis_records.stimulus: Requirement to add a new federate (e.g., new sensor model)
analysis_records.reasoning: By adhering to a standardized FOM and the HLA interface specification, the internal logic of the new federate is decoupled from the rest of the system, allowing for parallel development and rapid integration.

risks.rationale: If the Federation Object Model is overly complex or poorly documented, the 10-day target may be missed due to time spent mapping internal data structures to the FOM.

non_risks.rationale: The federated approach ensures that adding a new weapon system does not require recompilation or modification of existing federates.

scenario_reference.text: A distributed participant console fails or disconnects; the central simulation engine continues execution without data loss or interruption.

analysis_records.stimulus: Participant console network disconnect or software crash
analysis_records.response_measure: Core simulation remains stable; no data loss in central engine
analysis_records.reasoning: The central simulation engine (server) does not depend on synchronous acknowledgments from participant consoles (clients) to progress simulation time, isolating the core from edge failures.

non_risks.rationale: The RTI's ability to detect and drop disconnected federates prevents a single console crash from hanging the entire federation.
